title: Post-Workout Recovery Smoothie (Chocolate Cherry)
metaDescription: Chocolate cherry recovery smoothie with 24g of protein and tart cherry — a classic post-workout recovery combo. Ready in 5 minutes.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: recovery smoothie, post workout recovery drink, chocolate cherry smoothie
ingredients: 1/2 cup milk (dairy or unsweetened almond milk)

steps.name: Combine
steps.text: Add tart cherry juice, milk, Greek yogurt, cocoa powder, frozen banana, and honey to a blender.

steps.name: Blend
steps.text: Blend on high for 30-45 seconds until smooth and creamy.

steps.name: Drink after training
steps.text: Pour into a glass and drink after your workout, ideally within the same window you'd normally reach for a recovery shake.

tips.question: Why tart cherry juice specifically?
tips.answer: Tart cherry is a common ingredient in recovery drinks among endurance athletes, largely for its antioxidant content — it's a popular choice rather than a guaranteed fix, and works well flavor-wise with chocolate.

tips.question: Is chocolate milk really used for recovery?
tips.answer: Yes — plain chocolate milk's roughly 3:1 carb-to-protein ratio is a widely cited recovery benchmark among athletes, which is the ratio this smoothie is built around using cocoa powder instead of pre-made chocolate milk.

tips.question: Can I make this without dairy?
tips.answer: Yes — swap the milk and Greek yogurt for a dairy-free milk and yogurt alternative, though total protein will be lower without the dairy base.

tips.question: Can I use fresh cherries instead of tart cherry juice?
tips.answer: Fresh sweet cherries work in a pinch, but tart cherry juice (usually Montmorency variety) is what most recovery-focused recipes specifically call for — sweet cherries have a different flavor and nutrient profile.

storyIntro: Chocolate milk has been an unofficial recovery drink among athletes for years, mostly for its carb-to-protein ratio — this leans into that idea, but swaps in tart cherry juice, a fixture in a lot of endurance athletes' post-training routines.
Cocoa powder and Greek yogurt do the chocolate-milk-adjacent job here, landing at 24g of protein, while the tart cherry adds both flavor and its own reputation in recovery circles.
storyNote: This isn't a substitute for adequate rest or overall nutrition — it's one recovery drink option among many, built around ingredients that show up often in athlete recovery routines, not a guaranteed fix for soreness.
